title: Chapter 5 Master Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of for loops, while loops, and loop control.

questions.question: What does a for loop iterate over?
questions.explanation: for loops work with any iterable: lists, strings, ranges, tuples, dictionaries, etc.

questions.question: What does range(5) produce?
questions.explanation: range(5) generates 0 through 4 — it starts at 0 and stops BEFORE 5.

questions.question: range() includes the stop value.
questions.explanation: range() goes up to but NOT including the stop value.

questions.question: What does range(2, 8, 2) produce?
questions.explanation: Starts at 2, stops before 8, step 2: gives 2, 4, 6.

questions.question: How do you count backwards from 5 to 1?
questions.explanation: range(5,0,-1) starts at 5, stops before 0, stepping -1: 5,4,3,2,1.

questions.question: When does a while loop stop?
questions.explanation: while loops check their condition before each iteration and stop when it's False.

questions.question: Forgetting to update the loop variable can cause an infinite loop.
questions.explanation: If the condition never becomes False (because the variable isn't updated), the loop runs forever.

questions.question: What does break do?
questions.explanation: break immediately terminates the entire loop.

questions.question: What does continue do?
questions.explanation: continue skips the remaining code in the current iteration and moves to the next.

questions.question: When does a loop's else clause run?
questions.explanation: The else clause runs only when the loop finishes naturally (no break).

questions.question: Total iterations for: for i in range(3): for j in range(4)?
questions.explanation: Nested loops multiply: 3 × 4 = 12 total iterations.

questions.question: for _____ in range(10): loops 10 times.
questions.explanation: The loop variable can be any valid name. 'i' is convention but not required.

questions.question: How many times does 'for c in "Hello":' loop?
questions.explanation: "Hello" has 5 characters, so the loop runs 5 times.

questions.question: A for loop can iterate over a string character by character.
questions.explanation: Strings are sequences, so for loops iterate one character at a time.

questions.question: What's better for looping 100 times?
questions.explanation: for with range is cleaner and less error-prone for known iteration counts.

questions.question: What is the output of: for i in range(3): print(i)?
questions.explanation: range(3) produces 0, 1, 2 — each printed on its own line.

questions.question: To exit a loop early, use the _____ keyword.
questions.explanation: break is the keyword to immediately exit a loop.

questions.question: What happens if while True: has no break?
questions.explanation: while True with no break creates an infinite loop — it never stops.

questions.question: Nested loops should generally be avoided beyond 3 levels deep.
questions.explanation: Deep nesting causes exponential performance issues and poor readability.

questions.question: What does range(0, 10, 3) produce?
questions.explanation: Starts at 0, stops before 10, step 3: gives 0, 3, 6, 9.

questions.question: Which loop should you use when iterations are unknown?
questions.explanation: while loops are ideal when you don't know how many times to iterate.

questions.question: To skip the current iteration, use the _____ keyword.
questions.explanation: continue skips the rest of the current iteration and moves to the next one.
